free_text: Gamma Radiation from the Inelastic Scattering of
Protons by 19F
Thin evaporated targets of CaF2 on 0.004-in copper
backings.
The scintillation counter employed a 1-in.
diam x 1/2-in thick NaI(Tl) crystal. A 1/4-inch thick
quartz window let out the light from the
scintillations. The mounted crystal was then cemented
to a Dumont 6292 photomultiplier tube with Dow
Corning high-viscosity silicone oil.
In most of the runs during these
experiments, the number of counts was determined for
a fixed quantity of proton charge collected by the
target, as measured by a calibrated current
integrator. An electron suppressor was employed to
remove extraneous electrons from the slits defining
the incoming proton beam. The energy scale of the
scintillation counter was calibrated by observing the
"photopeaks" from the 177Lu gamma rays of energies
112.97 and 208.36 keV. During the study of excitation
curves, readings of yields were taken at both 0 and 90
degrees with respect to the incident beam direction.
At most resonances 45 and 135 degrees intensities
were also measured.
The excitation curves of the radiation,
following the inelastic proton scattering were
analyzed. Most of the resonances have been previously
identified as resonances for the production of
capture gamma radiation. In addition to the gamma-ray
yield, the absolute cross section for 109 and 197 keV
states in 19F was calculated using the scintillator
efficiency and stopping cross section. Angular
anisotropy of gamma-rays was consistent with the
previously determined spin and parity assignments.
BP
OS. Data correction in subs.8-11
On. Major alterations in 009 and 011.
